sub_topics.title: Types of Books of Prime Entry
competences: Record Business Transactions in the Books of Prime Entry
Post Business Transactions to the Ledger

instructional_blocks.hook: ACE Suppliers Limited is a small electronic wholesaler, whose transactions for the month of March 2025 are shown. With the aid of a chart showing types of Books of Prime Entry: a) Identify the Book of Prime Entry in which each transaction will be recorded. b) Explain each of the Books of Prime Entry identified.
teacher_steps: On a chart, draw a table showing types of Books of Prime Entry and their corresponding transactions recorded
Display the chart in class
Give learners the scenario and questions
Clear any misconceptions after learners' presentations
learner_steps: Discuss the two questions in their respective groups

instructional_blocks.hook: State the Source Documents for each Book of Prime Entry
teacher_steps: Organize various sample Source Documents (Invoice, Credit Note, Debit Note, Receipt, Bank Statement, Cheque Counterfoil, Cheque and Petty Cash Voucher)
On a chart, draw a table showing Source Documents, their purpose and corresponding Books of Prime Entry
Display the chart
Put learners in groups
Distribute sample Source Documents to groups
Ask learners to identify each source document, match with Book of Prime Entry, explain use, state content, fill in documents, and explain importance
Clear learners' misconceptions after presentations

sub_topics.short_notes: Books of Prime Entry are Books of Accounts used for recording different types of Business Transactions from various source documents. They include: 1) Purchases Day Book - goods bought on credit for resale; 2) Purchases Returns Day Book - goods bought on credit returned to suppliers; 3) Sales Day Book - goods sold on credit; 4) Sales Returns Day Book - goods sold on credit returned by customers; 5) Cash Book (One Column, Two Column, Three Column) - cash/bank receipts and payments; 6) Petty Cash Book - small cash expenditures; 7) General Journal - transactions that cannot be recorded in other Books of Prime Entry. Source Documents provide proof of transactions and include: Invoice (for credit purchases/sales), Credit Note (for returns/overcharges), Debit Note (buyer request for reduction), Receipt (proof of payment), Cheque, Bank Statement, and Petty Cash Voucher.

vocabulary.definition: Books in which business transactions are first recorded
Documents that show proof of a transaction
Acquisition of goods, services or assets by a firm for resale
The reduction in the price of items on the gross invoice price or list price for bulky buying
Goods bought on credit from suppliers for resale
Making a second entry in the ledger in order to complete the double entry system
A list of balances extracted from the Ledger

questions: Task 1: Identify and explain Books of Prime Entry for company transactions including cash/credit sales and purchases
Task 2: Identify where transactions should be recorded and explain identified Books of Prime Entry for QMD Enterprise
Task 3: Identify Book of Prime Entry for statements like 'Cash discount is shown in the...'
Task 4: Identify Books of Prime Entry for B & J Retailer and explain importance
Task 5: Explain types of transactions recorded in each Book of Prime Entry
Task 6: Complete table showing where transactions will be recorded

sub_topics.title: Purchases Day Book
competences: Record Business Transactions in the Books of Prime Entry - Purchases Day Book
Post Business Transactions to the Ledger

instructional_blocks.hook: Kay Gee Limited is a retailer that buys and sells goods both for cash and on credit. They have not kept proper record of Books of Prime Entry. As a qualified Accountant, prepare the Purchases Day Book for February 2025.
teacher_steps: Write credit purchases transactions on the board (at least 6 transactions)
Pick the first 2 transactions and demonstrate how to record them in Purchases Day Book, taking into account trade discount
Allow learners working in pairs to record remaining transactions
Mark exercise and correct each pair's work to clear misconceptions
Give individualized class exercise/homework
learner_steps: Work in pairs and prepare the Purchases Day Book from list of credit transactions

instructional_blocks.hook: TT Traders had challenge to establish total credit purchases. You have been engaged as Accounts Clerk to prepare Purchases Day Book, post to Ledger, and extract Trial Balance for January 2025.
teacher_steps: Write credit purchases transactions on board (at least 6 transactions)
Pick 2 accounts (one with multiple posting, another with single posting) and demonstrate recording in Accounts Payable Ledger
Allow learners in pairs to record remaining transactions
Mark learner's work and clear misconceptions
Give individualized class exercise/homework
learner_steps: Work in pairs and prepare General Ledger and Accounts Payable Ledger from example
Copy exercise/homework and continue hands-on during free time

instructional_blocks.hook: From Ledger Accounts, extract the Trial Balance
teacher_steps: From Ledger Accounts in Learning Activity 5, allow learners to extract Trial Balance working in pairs
Mark learner's presentations and clear misconceptions
learner_steps: Continue working in pairs and extract Trial Balance
Copy exercise/homework and continue hands-on during free time

sub_topics.short_notes: The Purchases Day Book is one of the Books of Prime Entry where all goods bought on credit for resale are recorded. Also known as Purchases Journal, Creditors or Bought Journal. Source document is the Original Invoice. Format includes: Date, Details, Invoice No., Sub-total, Total Amount columns. The total value of each Purchases Invoice is posted to credit side of each Supplier's Account in Purchases Ledger. Total of Purchases Day Book is posted to debit side of Purchases Account in General Ledger. Trade Discount is reduction in list price for bulky buying - used when calculating net invoice price, does not enter accounting records. Cash Discount is reduction for prompt payment (Discount Allowed to customers, Discount Received from suppliers).

vocabulary.definition: Goods bought on credit from suppliers for resale
Making a second entry in the ledger to complete the double entry system
A list of balances extracted from the Ledger

questions: Task 1: Prepare Purchases Day Book, post to Ledger, extract Trial Balance for Bulonga Stores (January 2025)
Task 2: Prepare Purchases Day Book, post to Ledger, extract Trial Balance for Palauka Enterprise (March 2025)
Task 3: Prepare Purchases Day Book, post to Ledger, extract Trial Balance for Star Enterprise (January 2025)
Task 4: Prepare Purchases Day Book, post to Ledger, extract Trial Balance for VXC Traders (December 2024)
Task 5: Prepare Purchases Day Book, post to Ledger, extract Trial Balance for Mbwete General Dealers (February 2025)
Task 6: Prepare Purchases Day Book, post to Ledger, extract Trial Balance for TMN Enterprise (November 2024)
Task 7: Prepare Purchases Day Book, post to Ledger, extract Trial Balance for Mweemba retailer (January 2025)

sub_topics.title: Purchases Returns Day Book
competences: Record Business Transactions in the Books of Prime Entry - Purchases Returns Day Book
Post Business Transactions to the Ledger

instructional_blocks.hook: TT Traders Limited had some debtors that returned goods during January 2025. You have been requested as qualified Accountant to prepare the Purchases Returns Journal from the transactions.
teacher_steps: Write credit purchases returns transactions on board (at least 2)
Pick first 2 transactions and demonstrate recording in Purchases Returns Journal
Allow learners in pairs to record remaining transactions
Mark exercise and correct each pair's work to clear misconceptions
Give individualized class exercise/homework
learner_steps: Work in pairs and prepare Purchases Returns Journal
Copy homework and continue hands-on during free time

instructional_blocks.hook: Refer to Learning Activity 4 to post transactions to Ledger
teacher_steps: Pick first part demonstrated on and post to Purchases Ledger, show Purchases Returns account in General Ledger
Allow learners in pairs to post remaining part
Mark their work and clear learners' misconceptions
learner_steps: Work in pairs and post Purchases Returns Day Book to appropriate Ledgers

instructional_blocks.hook: Refer to previous work in Learning Activity 5 for posting to Ledger
teacher_steps: Pick one item and demonstrate extraction of Trial Balance
Allow learners to finish extracting Trial Balance and balance it up
Mark learners' books and clear misconceptions
Give homework
learner_steps: Work in pairs and finish extracting Trial Balance

sub_topics.short_notes: Purchases Returns Day Book (Returns Outwards Journal/Day Book) is where goods once bought but later returned are recorded due to reasons like wrong colour, wrong order, wrong sizes. Source document is Original Credit Note. Format: Date, Details, Credit Note No., Subtotal, Total Amount columns. Posting rules: Debit all Accounts Payable (Creditors Accounts) in Purchases Ledger with total amounts returned. Transfer total of Purchases Returns Journal to credit side of Purchases Returns Account in General Ledger to complete double entry.

vocabulary.definition: Goods that were bought and now returned to the Seller

questions: Task 1: Prepare Purchases Returns Day Book, post to Ledgers, extract Trial Balance for EMK (January 2025)
Task 2: Prepare Purchases Returns Journal, post to Ledgers, extract Trial Balance for J P Limited (March 2025)
Task 3: Prepare Purchases Returns Day Book, post to Ledger, extract Trial Balance for TJ Enterprises (April 2025)
Task 4: Prepare Purchases Returns Day Book, post to Ledger, extract Trial Balance for Paka Stores (December 2024)
Task 5: Prepare Purchases Returns Day Book, Ledger Books, Trial Balance for Maccha Retailers (February 2025)
